baseline_review.requirement: Exact statement/status, historical and current methods, closest work, formal repository, tooling, compute scale, and external acceptance path audited.

synthesis_summary: Problem #530 remains open on the maintained source. The scoped Lean candidate and finite semantic controls remain intact. Run 2 showed that the saved bare numeric Lake target and a --dir-relative source target both fail during target addressing, while a direct current-cache attempt failed before source elaboration because the bootstrap support OLean was still being built. The correct disambiguated numeric target +FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.«530» is now queued as the one-segment warning-fatal lab job lab-scout-bdf77f43574b-eec7321621e3; queueing is not build evidence.

established_facts.claim: The maintained page states the min-max Sidon-subset problem over finite subsets of the reals, asks whether ell(N) is asymptotic to sqrt(N), marks it OPEN, and says it is not formalised.
established_facts.scope: Maintained-source status, not a literature-complete certification

established_facts.claim: Formal Conjectures issue #773 is open, unassigned, up for grabs, and has no linked development branch or pull request.
Bailleul and Riblet report (1/(3 sqrt 3)+o(1)) sqrt(n) as a lower bound for arbitrary finite subsets of Z and R^d.
established_facts.scope: Claim reported by the preprint; proof not independently reconstructed in this epoch

established_facts.claim: The literal four-variable Sidon predicate agrees with uniqueness of unordered pair sums with repetition on every subset of {-4,...,4}.
established_facts.scope: Every subset of the stated nine-element integer domain only

established_facts.claim: At Lake 5.0.0 / Lean 4.27.0, the bare CLI target FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.530 fails with an empty-path no-such-file error before candidate elaboration; Lake's documented disambiguated numeric module form is +FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.«530».
established_facts.scope: CLI target addressing in the pinned Formal Conjectures workspace checkout; not a claim about the Lean source

strategy_registry.mechanism: Translate the maintained statement using Finset, Nat.findGreatest, and Mathlib asymptotic equivalence; attack semantics with finite controls before pinned elaboration.
strategy_registry.first_discriminator: Inspect lab-scout-bdf77f43574b-eec7321621e3, which runs the warning-fatal disambiguated numeric target build; only after it passes submit the full warning-fatal build.

open_leads.description: Elaborate the candidate in a current Formal Conjectures checkout.
open_leads.rationale: Kernel elaboration is the cheapest decisive test of the contribution.
open_leads.next_experiment: Inspect the completed record for lab-scout-bdf77f43574b-eec7321621e3 without resubmitting; on exit 0, submit a separate full lake --wfail build.

open_leads.description: Obtain human review of the exact-size extremal definition and asymptotic translation.
open_leads.rationale: Kernel checking cannot establish equivalence to the informal source statement.
open_leads.next_experiment: Request assignment on issue #773, submit a linked PR after local build, and ask reviewers to check four enumerated semantic points in CHECKPOINT.md.

open_leads.description: Resolve the 0.08 versus 0.0805 description of Abbott's prior constant.
open_leads.rationale: The discrepancy affects only the literature note, not the Lean target, but should not be propagated.
open_leads.next_experiment: Compare Abbott's proof optimization with the exact passage and citation in arXiv:2605.03181v1.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Address numeric problem 530 as bare Lake target FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.530 or as a --dir-relative source path.
ruled_out.scope: Lake 5.0.0 target addressing at the pinned Lean 4.27.0 checkout
ruled_out.reason: The bare target gives an empty-path no-such-file failure and the relative source path is unknown; both fail before Lean elaboration. The documented disambiguated form is +FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.«530».
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Only a Lake version change whose documented target grammar makes either form valid.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Use weak Sidon uniqueness that ignores repeated summands.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Only if an authoritative source explicitly changes the definition to weak Sidon.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Demand uniqueness of ordered pair representations.
ruled_out.reason: It wrongly rejects every nontrivial swapped pair (a,b)/(b,a).
ruled_out.reopen_condition: None for the present source statement.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat the maintained page's 2026-04-08 literature note as current-complete.
ruled_out.reason: arXiv:2605.03181 was submitted afterward and gives a newer lower-bound constant.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Not applicable; future audits must search past the page edit date.

next_session.objective: Turn the drafted interface into a kernel-checked, externally reviewed Formal Conjectures contribution.
next_session.first_action: Inspect records/labs for lab-scout-bdf77f43574b-eec7321621e3; do not resubmit the already queued correct-target build.
next_session.stop_condition: If the job passes, submit the full warning-fatal build; otherwise preserve the first dependency, warning, or elaboration discrepancy. Terminal success still requires target build, full build, CI, and human semantic review.

current_facts: Mathlib's Nat.findGreatest supplies a bounded finite extremum and maximality lemmas.
Mathlib's Asymptotics.IsEquivalent notation directly represents the source's tilde relation.
current_exclusions: Weak Sidon and ordered-pair uniqueness are semantically wrong for #530.
A larger finite cutoff is not a contribution and will not replace Lean elaboration.
current_leads: Formal extremal definitions in Mathlib such as the Ruzsa-Szemeredi number predict that Nat.findGreatest is reviewer-familiar; test by pinned elaboration and review.

linked_problems.source_concept: Mathlib Ruzsa-Szemeredi extremal number uses Nat.findGreatest
linked_problems.prediction: A bounded Nat.findGreatest definition for ell will have a short maximality API and fit library idiom.
linked_problems.test: Pinned target build plus Formal Conjectures review

linked_problems.prediction: A weak-Sidon predicate would pass distinct-pair checks but fail a double-summand control.
linked_problems.status: observed: weak predicate accepts it, so transfer rejected for #530
